title: Your Life as a Hitman at Every Level (You Don't Retire)
hook: You owe $18,000 you can't pay, and the men holding that debt offer you a way to work it off. The first name is free. By the time you understand what this life actually costs, you already have too many names to walk away from any of them.
body: POV: you're a hitman, climbing a real, documented ladder of contract killing one name and one number at a time. THE DEBT: $0 — you owe $18,000 to men who round the interest up like a hobby, and your mother's hospital bill is why. THE FIRST NAME: $5,000, the one that never leaves you. THE JOURNEYMAN: $25,000, about the real-world average fee for an actual documented contract killing — roughly the price of a used car, paid for a life. THE CLEANER: $60,000, no witnesses, no traces. THE SPECIALIST: $250,000, for a name that turns out to be your own mentor's. THE GHOST: $1,000,000, a client who doesn't do paperwork. THE HANDLER: you stop pulling the trigger and start taking 12% of everyone else's instead. THE OPEN CONTRACT: $2,000,000 — the price, it turns out, is on you. Real, verified mechanics woven into the story: a documented UK study of 27 real contract killings found an average fee of about $20,000 (fees ranged as low as $200), and a real four-tier hierarchy — novice, dilettante, journeyman, master; the FBI works 70-90 murder-for-hire cases a year, and the 'professional' most people find online is, more often than not, a cop running a sting. This is a dramatized cautionary POV about organized crime, not a how-to — the real lesson is that nobody in this life gets to just retire. Watch the whole series — every life, every level, every way the climb costs you. For educational and entertainment purposes; characters are fictional, the documented facts are real.
pinned: The real average fee for a documented contract killing is about $20,000 — the price of a used car. Which level do you think breaks first: the money, or the mentor?
poll: Where does this life get you? THE FIRST NAME (you're in) / THE SPECIALIST (you kill your own mentor) / THE HANDLER (you stop pulling the trigger) / THE OPEN CONTRACT (the price is on you)
